pages.title: How I Can Help - Satvir Kaur MP
pages.text_excerpt: How I Can Help - Satvir Kaur MP Skip to the content Satvir Kaur MP Fighting for Southampton Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Menu Close Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Facebook Twitter Instagram How I Can Help As your Member of Parliament, there are different things I can do to represent and help you. Please note: I can only help people in Southampton Test Due to strict parliamentary protocol, I can only help people that live in my constituency, Southampton Test. Please enter your postcode here to check that you live in Southampton Test. Please also provide your full name and full postal address including postcode when you contact me. I have a problem and need assistance. Can you help? A big part of the job of an MP is helping constituents who are in difficulty. On behalf of my constituents, I can contact Government departments, agencies, local councils, utility companies, housing associations and many more in order to raise issues, chase up enquiries and advocate for people. If you require help with an issue to do with benefits, housing, education, asylum and immigration, health, or something else, please do get in touch and me and my team will be able to assist you. How can I tell you how I feel about a particular policy? Can you help me with a question about policy? If you have an opinion you want to share or a question about policy, I would love to hear from you and will always respond to your concerns. Depending on what you want to know or say, I will also write to the relevant Government minister on your behalf to share your opinion or to seek clarity on a particular point. I can also ask Written and Oral Questions in Parliament. I want to make you aware of something going on in Southampton Test. How do I go about doing that? I love talking all things Southampton and want to hear from you about anything, good or bad, that you want to raise in the constituency. Whether you have a brilliant local project you want to make me aware of, or if there is an issue in the constituency you want to raise, I want to know about it. I would rather speak to you in person. How do I go about booking a surgery appointment? If you would like to speak to me directly about any or all of the above, you can book to meet me at a surgery appointment . I hold regular surgeries for constituents in need and I am always happy to chat and listen to your concerns. You can book an appointment by contacting my office. I want to speak to you about something else not listed here. Is that OK? If you are in any doubt about what I can and cannot help you with, please contact my office who will be able to advise you. MPs have an area of responsibility that we have to work within, so some things will fall outside of that, but please ask if you are not sure. CONTACT SATVIR Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns About Me Values News Contact Facebook Twitter Instagram © 2026 Satvir Kaur MP Privacy Policy Powered by WordPress To the top ↑ Up ↑

pages.title: In Parliament - Satvir Kaur MP
pages.text_excerpt: In Parliament - Satvir Kaur MP Skip to the content Satvir Kaur MP Fighting for Southampton Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Menu Close Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Facebook Twitter Instagram In Parliament My record in Parliament For everything you need to know about what I have been up to Parliament, including my Voting Record, Spoken Contributions, Written Questions and Registered Interests, please visit my page on Parliament.uk or click below. MY RECORD ON PARLIAMENT.UK My expenses The Independent Parliamentary Standards Authority (IPSA) is the independent body that regulates and administers the business costs and decides the pay and pensions of MPs and their staff. The expense and allowance claims for individual MPs are published regularly and can be viewed on the IPSA website. You can access details of my claims here or via the button below. pages.title: About Me - Satvir Kaur MP
pages.meta_description: Find out more about Satvir. Read about her background, early life, education, jobs and political career to date.
pages.text_excerpt: About Me - Satvir Kaur MP Skip to the content Satvir Kaur MP Fighting for Southampton Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Menu Close Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Facebook Twitter Instagram About Me I love Southampton, but there is still so much we can all do to improve our great city. Born and bred Southampton, I’ve seen the change we can make, working together to build on the achievements of previous generations. Growing up in Southampton I have lived in Southampton all my life. I grew up on free school meals in the inner city, was educated here, and am immensely proud to have graduated from both the University of Southampton and Solent University, first in History and Politics and then in Law. I’ve never forgotten my roots. My home was in Newtown, where my family still live. Growing up in one of the most deprived areas in the city came with its challenges but I loved it. I learned the importance of community, relying on good public services such as libraries and youth centres, and I discovered there is real strength in our diversity. I have carried that early experience throughout my life. Every child needs opportunities and a thriving community to live a life of joy, fulfilment and dignity. That must start with education. Education is the most important opportunity there is. My life was changed by attending St Anne’s, a good local state school where I was Head Girl. I believe we never stop learning, and should always try to grow our knowledge, understanding, skills and empathy. I have tried to live this throughout my life in politics. Employment and experience My previous jobs have given me experience that I carry through to my politics. I have worked for a charity and in a major publishing company, but the most important lessons were the ones I learnt as the daughter of a market trader, and then helping with the family fabric business. I learnt the economic and social importance of small business and local highstreets, and how hard we all had to work to keep the business afloat. And sometimes, having a market trader’s shout is pretty useful for debating against the Tories. Political work I joined the Labour Party because I wanted to make a real difference to my community and the city I love and, in 2011, I was elected to Southampton City Council in Shirley, a then-safe Tory ward. We have built a great local campaign team which responds to residents’ needs and gets our message out there. As councillor for Shirley ward and cabinet member within the city for over a decade, I saw first-hand the difference Labour values and policies made to Shirley and our city and it was an honour to help deliver these. From setting up initiatives to tackle poverty and inequalities, protecting and building community infrastructure, to ensuring we all have a home that is safe, secure and affordable . We achieved so much, but I know much more needs to be done. I was also responsible for launching the bid for Southampton to become UK City of Culture 2025 , a competition in which we were finalists. It was a process that transformed our city and created a sense of belonging and pride. During the brief period of Conservative control in Southampton following the 2021 local elections, I became Leader of the Labour Group and Opposition in the city. Whilst in this role I continued to help put Southampton on the map and influenced national policy in a variety of areas. Leading the Council With Labour taking back control of the Council in the 2022 Local

headings: h2: Southampton MPs urge city’s renters to ‘Know your Rights’ as new laws come into force from today

pages.text_excerpt: News - Satvir Kaur MP Skip to the content Satvir Kaur MP Fighting for Southampton Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Menu Close Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Facebook Twitter Instagram Categories Information News Southampton MPs urge city’s renters to ‘Know your Rights’ as new laws come into force from today Post author By Satvir Post date 01/05/2026 Southampton MPs have today hailed a landmark moment for renters in the city as new rights for tenants come into force. Satvir Kaur, MP for Southampton Test, and Darren Paffey, MP for Southampton Itchen, are encouraging the thousands of renters in the city to make sure that they are aware of their new rights as a result of the government’s Renters Rights Act. Under the new laws that come into force today: Landlords will no longer be able to make evictions for no reason with a Section 21 notice, putting an end to revenge evictions for tenants who make legitimate requests for repairs or complaints about conditions. Tenants cannot be made to pay rent more than one month in advance Landlords can only put the rent up once a year and they must give at least two months’ notice. Tenants will also be able to go to a rent tribunal if they think the rent is too much. It will be illegal to refuse tenants just because they receive benefits or have kids. Councils are also being given new powers to crack down on rogue landlords. Landlords will still be able to get their properties back for clear and justifiable reasons – like selling up, moving in, or dealing with rent arrears and antisocial behaviour. Continue reading “Southampton MPs urge city’s renters to ‘Know your Rights’ as new laws come into force from today” Tags darren paffey , labour government , member of parliament , mp , no fault evictions , renters' rights act , satvir kaur , southampton , southampton test Categories Information What it means to have a Labour Council working with a Labour Government Post author By Satvir Post date 30/04/2026 Satvir Kaur MP with Leader of Southampton City Council, Councillor Alex Winning It’s election time again, with one week to go until Southampton goes to the polls. You may have received your ballot papers already, if you vote by post. This time around, we’ll be choosing who we want representing us on our City Council. It’s an important choice, and one that should be carefully considered, but it is a choice that I believe should always be made. The opportunity to vote is a fundamental human right and one that has been hard fought for by many. Decisions made by politicians have an enormous impact on our lives, whether taken at a national level – by the Government and by MPs – or at a local level, by councillors and the local Executive. For the first time in a very long time, we have a Labour Council in Southampton working hand-in-hand with a Labour Government in Westminster. The benefits of this partnership cannot be overstated and, for the first time since 2010 – when Conservative and Lib Dem austerity took a wrecking ball to city coffers – we are seeing local authorities receive funding based on local need . With Labour, money allocated to councils is tied to local levels of deprivation, rather than favouring a particular political stripe, as we suffered previously. Continue reading “What it means to have a Labour Council working with a Labour Government” Tags council elections , labour , labour government , local elections , may 7th , member of parliament , satvir kaur , southampton , southampt

pages.title: Contact - Satvir Kaur MP
pages.meta_description: Have a question, comment or enquiry? Get in touch with Satvir and share your views via the contact form on this page.
pages.text_excerpt: Contact - Satvir Kaur MP Skip to the content Satvir Kaur MP Fighting for Southampton Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Menu Close Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Facebook Twitter Instagram Contact Please do get in touch with me and my team if there is anything I can ever assist you with. Whether it be giving your opinion on policy, sharing ideas about making Southampton a better place, or asking for help in hard times, my door is always open and my first priority is serving you. Please note, strict Parliamentary rules say that MPs can only help their own constituents. Please visit this website and enter your postcode to check if I am your MP. Please make sure you have included your full name, address and contact details in your correspondence. Contact Details Postal Address: Satvir Kaur MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Email: satvir.kaur.mp@parliament.uk Phone: 02382 546 357 🗞️ SIGN UP TO MY NEWSLETTER 🗞️ A note about acceptable communication I receive hundreds of emails from constituents each day and my constituency office staff speak to many people over the phone every week. I also interact with lots of people via my social media pages and in Facebook groups. Whilst the vast majority of communication is civil and respectful, even if we are in profound disagreement, occasionally an email, phone conversation, or social media comment can tip from being impassioned to being disrespectful or even abusive. In these instances – particularly when racist, sexist, homophobic, personal, offensive or derogatory language is used – I will take a zero-tolerance approach. The wellbeing of my staff is my utmost priority and I reserve the right to cease communication with those concerned, whether by ending a phone call, not replying to an email, hiding or deleting social media posts or comments, or blocking repeat offenders. Thank you so much to all who contact me, I really appreciate hearing views from across the political spectrum and think there is huge value in respectful debate. This is just a small plea, from me and my team, to make sure we keep it respectful, for the benefit of all.
